title: podman pull -> higher CPU usage than docker pull
body:
### Issue Description

Pulling image via Podman API has higher CPU usage than via Docker API on the same machine ( Fedora CoreOS , both docker and podman are installed by default )

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
```
echo '{"max-concurrent-uploads": 20}' > /etc/docker/daemon.json

cat << EOF > /etc/containers/containers.conf
[engine]
image_parallel_copies=20
EOF

systemctl restart docker
systemctl restart podman

pidstat -l -G "^/usr/bin/podman --log-level=info system service" 1 |  grep -v '%CPU' > podman.csv
time curl -X POST --unix-socket /run/podman/podman.sock http://d/v4/libpod/images/pull?reference=docker.io%2Fsagemathinc%2Fcocalc%3A8b4a75fbfde8

pidstat -l -G "^/usr/bin/dockerd" 1 |  grep -v '%CPU' > docker.csv
time curl -X POST --unix-socket /var/run/docker.sock http://d/images/create?fromImage=docker.io%2Fsagemathinc%2Fcocalc%3A8b4a75fbfde8
```

### Describe the results you received

## docker
real	time 2m6.423s ( curl ) 
avg CPU 129% ( pidstat )

## podman 
real	time 2m6.293s ( curl ) 
avg CPU 274%  ( pidstat )

### Describe the results you expected

avg CPU is ~same as docker pull

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Fedora CoreOS 37.20230312.20.0

### Additional information

- pidstat data ( docker ) - https://pastebin.com/raw/3TU7YL6d
- pidstat data ( podman ) - https://pastebin.com/raw/8KyjMy2k

comments.body: The Podman application and docker daemon are doing very different things, I have no idea why their is bigger CPU overhead in Podmna, but this is somewhat comparing Apples and Oranges.
I know.., but with the same attitude, I believe I can say that Podman has higher HW requirements for most of the users moving from Docker, right?

Sure, but unlike docker, podman goes away when a dameonized service starts. It does not need to linger in the back ground.
Also docker includes two daemons dockerd and containerd.  Are you measuring both?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since I never heard back closing.  Feel free to continue the conversation here.
